events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON. Need identify component/condition: harness impact indicator deployed (webbing tag). This indicates harness has been subjected to fall arrest force and must be removed from service. Condition: installed-defective? It's a personal fall arrest harness with deployed impact indicator. Since work rough-complete, harness present maybe in use. Defect cues: impact indicator webbing tag deployed/extended, sign of shock load. Decision fail, risk high or critical? A deployed impact indicator means harness no longer reliable; if used for fall protection, critical. But maybe just observed condition. component_conditions: installed-defective, failed, non-compliant. Need include findings and actions. References: OSHA 1926 Subpart M (personal fall arrest systems), 1910.140(c)(18) maybe? Actually 1910.140(c)(18): personal fall protection equipment removed from service if subjected to impact loading. Also 1926.95(d)(1), 1926 Subpart M 1926.502(d)(19). Need produce JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision":{"decision":"fail","risk":"critical","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","failed","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":85,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Personal fall-arrest harness impact indicator (webbing tag) is deployed/extended","Harness has been subjected to shock-loading and is no longer fit for service","Defective harness remains in circulation/installed on the worker or at the work area"],"actions":["Immediately remove the harness from service and tag it as DO NOT USE","Quarantine the harness for destruction or manufacturer evaluation; do not reissue","Inspect all other harnesses on site for deployed impact indicators and proper condition","Provide a compliant, inspected harness before work at height resumes","Brief crew on impact-indicator recognition and removal-from-service requirements"],"rationale":"A deployed impact-indicator tag on a harness webbing strap is visual evidence that the harness has arrested a fall or been shock-loaded. OSHA requires personal fall protection equipment subjected to impact loading to be removed from service immediately. Using or continuing to use such a harness creates a critical fall-arrest failure risk.","references":["OSHA 1926 Subpart M - Fall Protection","OSHA 1910.140(c)(18) - Personal fall protection systems and components removed from service after impact loading","OSHA 1926.502(d) - Personal fall arrest systems inspection and use criteria"]}}
